text: 0.7g of fat. saturated fat, cholesterol, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. the rest is not doing much. in this category, carbs barely matter.
17.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 54% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

on the plus side: gives a straightforward protein solution because the category often wins on simplicity and reliability. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat.

the drawbacks next: fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. the outcome depends heavily on preparation because a good raw profile can still be cooked into a worse result. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count.
A tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
